name: Spinach Empanadas
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese and butter together until smooth (use a stand mixer as the mixture is heavy). Gradually add in the flour and salt. Lightly knead the dough by hand. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 3 hours.
2. In a medium skillet, cook the onion and garlic in the bacon drippings until the onion is tender but not brown. Mix in the cooked and crumbled bacon, thawed and drained spinach, cottage cheese, pepper, and nutmeg; let the mixture cool.
3. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Roll out the pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of 1/8 inch. Cut 3-inch circles in the pastry, place 1 teaspoon of the filling in each circle, just off center. Moisten the edge of the circle with egg, fold the dough over the filling to make a semicircular empanada. Seal the edges with fork tines and poke the top of each pastry with a fork to vent.
4. Place the empanadas on an ungreased cookie sheet, brush their tops with egg, and bake for 10 to 12 minutes or until golden. Makes 60 empanadas.
